response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. To get through a leap year, she needs to calculate the total amount of shampoo required. A leap year has 366 days, so she will use shampoo 366 ÷ 2 = 183 times. This means she needs 183 × (1/4) = 45.75 ounces of shampoo. Since shampoo is sold in 14-ounce bottles, she will need to buy enough bottles to cover 45.75 ounces. This means she will need at least 4 bottles, as 3 bottles would only provide 42 ounces, and 4 bottles provide 56 ounces, which is more than enough.

[total_shampoo_needed] is the total amount of shampoo required for the leap year. [bottle_size] is the size of each shampoo bottle. She will need to buy [number_of_bottles] bottles to cover her needs.
